name: Default Accounts Used
notes:
This rule detects default accounts used.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali
SIGMA Title: Suspicious Manipulation Of Default Accounts
SIGMA Description: Detects suspicious manipulations of default accounts such as 'administrator' and 'guest'. For example 'enable' or 'disable' accounts or change the password...etc


Confidence: HIGH

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success

when the event matches ( LOWER("Process Full Full Name") == 'net.exe' OR LOWER("Process Full Full Name") == 'net1.exe' ) AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '% user %' AND ( "Command" MATCHES '.*?[''"]{0,1}(Järjestelmänvalvoja|Rendszergazda|Администратор|Administrateur|Administrador|Administratör|Administrator|guest|DefaultAccount)[''"]{0,1}.*?' ) AND NOT ( LOWER("Command") LIKE '%guest%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%/active no%' ) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Default Accounts Used on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success

AND when the event matches ( 	LOWER("Process Full Full Name") == 'net.exe'  	OR LOWER("Process Full Full Name") == 'net1.exe' ) AND  	LOWER("Command") LIKE '% user %' AND  ( 	"Command" MATCHES '.*?[''"]{0,1}(Järjestelmänvalvoja|Rendszergazda|Администратор|Administrateur|Administrador|Administratör|Administrator|guest|DefaultAccount)[''"]{0,1}.*?' ) AND NOT ( 	LOWER("Command") LIKE '%guest%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%/active no%' ) AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: Detects default accounts used.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
